vocab.meaning: tái đầu tư (lợi nhuận) vào doanh nghiệp
vocab.example: Entrepreneurs use banks to manage their cash flow, allowing them to plough back profits into their expanding businesses.

vocab.meaning: xóa nợ (khi không còn khả năng thu hồi)
vocab.example: Some people hope the bank will write off their debts during extreme financial crises, though this is rarely the case.

vocab.meaning: ngăn chặn một điều tồi tệ xảy ra (thường là rủi ro tài chính)
vocab.example: Individuals often keep an emergency fund in a bank to stave off potential bankruptcy during a job loss.

vocab.meaning: tiêu lạm vào tiền tiết kiệm
vocab.example: Having a savings account is convenient, but it’s often too easy to dip into those funds for impulsive purchases.
People take out personal loans from banks to bail themselves out when they face unexpected medical expenses.

vocab.meaning: miễn cưỡng trả một khoản tiền
vocab.example: Banks provide a secure way to pay bills, so customers don't have to cough up large sums of cash in person.

vocab.meaning: tổng hợp, tính toán tổng số tiền
vocab.example: Mobile banking apps help users tally up their monthly expenditures with just a few taps on the screen.

vocab.meaning: thu về rất nhiều tiền (thường là lợi nhuận/lãi suất)
vocab.example: Savvy investors use banks to rake in interest from fixed-term deposits over several years.

vocab.meaning: củng cố, làm cho vững chắc hơn (tài chính)
vocab.example: Taking out a small business loan can help a startup shore up its finances during the initial months of operation.

vocab.meaning: nợ nần chồng chất (do chi tiêu quá mức)
vocab.example: Credit cards provided by banks are useful, but they can lead people to run up massive debts if not managed carefully.
